_note: Manually update 'outcome' and 'outcome_note' as events resolve. outcome: null=pending, 'bull'=positive signal, 'bear'=negative signal, 'neutral'=mixed/in-line. static_gauges is intentionally empty — dealer gamma, DDR4 spot, and TSMC revenue removed (no free API); P/C OI, constituent corr, and HMM stress are now auto-computed by update_data.py. See WORKLOG.md entry 2026-07-02 for full rationale.

events.watch: Any upside surprise → yield spike → SOXX same-day drag
events.bull: In-line or softer print; yields stable/falling
events.bear: Hot print + yields +8bp = likely −1 to −2% SOXX drag
events.action_bull: Removes macro headwind. No change to regime signal, but reduces near-term pressure.
events.action_bear: Hot print near a catalyst cluster = elevated crash risk. Confirms 4.7-4.8% TNX danger zone.

events.watch: NT$ revenue vs implied ~NT$422–443B to hit Q2 guide of $39–40.2B USD; Apr+May = NT$827.7B
events.bull: ≥NT$422B = demand intact, Q2 guide on track
events.action_bull: Q2 guide on track; fundamental demand floor intact. Does NOT change regime signal — tape still broken. Action: hold core, watch for +5% rally to trim into.
events.action_bear: First hard data point of demand softening. Adds fundamental reason to derisk beyond just tape/vol. Action: accelerate trimming on any bounce.

events.watch: C.C. Wei language on: HBM demand, AI server 2H26 visibility, 3nm/5nm utilization, any guide raise
events.bull: Raises 2H26 outlook; 'demand outpacing supply'; HBM allocated through 2027
events.bear: 'Visibility limited'; 'customers moderating'; any capex pull-back language
events.action_bull: Guidance raise narrative revives. Vol regime may begin to normalize — watch for RVol20 to start declining toward 60%. Action: begin rebuilding position if RVol also confirms.
events.action_bear: Broadcom pattern repeating (in-line but no raise = crash). Expect another −8 to −12% event. Action: be at reduced-core sizing BEFORE this event.

events.watch: Process control bookings, WFE spending outlook; Q3 FY26 revenue was $3.415B (+11.5% YoY)
events.bull: Bookings raised, WFE outlook upgraded again
events.action_bull: Equipment backlog extending — confirms AI infrastructure demand is real and durable.
events.action_bear: First sign of equipment demand softening. Leading indicator: book-to-bill declining 12–18 months before revenue impact.

events.watch: Google Cloud YoY growth (was +63% Q1), capex guidance vs $175–185B full year, AI infrastructure commentary
events.bear: Cloud deceleration; capex cut; efficiency-over-scale narrative
events.action_bull: Hyperscaler demand floor confirmed for one of four. Need all four to confirm for regime change.
events.action_bear: THIS IS THE THESIS-BREAK SCENARIO. At 42× fwd P/E with leverage in the basket, genuine capex reversal = 30–50% drawdown scenario. Action: sell, not trim.

events.watch: Azure growth (guided 39–40% CC), Q4 capex (guided >$40B), AI ARR growth (was $37B run rate, +123% YoY)
events.bull: Azure ≥39%; capex raised; AI ARR acceleration
events.bear: Azure miss; capex surprise cut; capacity commentary softens
events.action_bull: Second hyperscaler confirmed. Two of four checks.
events.action_bear: Capex cut from MSFT after GOOGL = consensus shift. Accelerate derisking.

events.watch: Rate decision + Powell language on cuts
events.bull: Cut or explicitly dovish guidance; yields fall
events.bear: Hold + hawkish; or 'higher for longer' language
events.action_bull: Tailwind for 42× fwd P/E SOXX. Supports regime normalization if tape also heals.
events.action_bear: Headwind. Sustained TNX above 4.7–4.8% would pressure SOXX meaningfully at current valuation.

events.watch: Capex guidance vs $125–145B (already raised from $115–135B), AI infra tone
events.bull: Capex raised again or held firm; ad revenue strong
events.bear: Capex cut; 'reassessing AI spend'; demand uncertainty
events.action_bull: Third hyperscaler confirmed. Three of four checks.
events.action_bear: META capex cut would be a major signal shift — they've been the most aggressive raiser.

events.watch: AWS growth (was 28% Q1), capex vs ~$200B full-year guide (Q1 alone was $44.2B), AI chip revenue ($20B run rate)
events.bull: AWS ≥28%; capex maintained; chip demand commentary strong
events.bear: AWS deceleration; capex cut (Amazon FCF already going negative)
events.action_bull: ALL FOUR HYPERSCALERS CONFIRMED: demand floor locked. Check TSMC + Micron + equipment names too. If all confirm: fundamental case to reduce derisk posture.
events.action_bear: Fourth cut = thesis broken. This is the event that argues for selling, not trimming.

events.watch: Memory deposition/etch demand, HBM equipment backlog; Q2 FY26 revenue was $7.91B (+11.4% YoY), guidance 'equipment growth >30%'
events.bull: HBM demand 'sold out into 2027'; backlog extends
events.action_bull: HBM equipment confirming demand is real. Leading indicator for MU/SK Hynix capacity.
events.action_bear: Memory equipment softening = HBM demand peak signal. Watch MU price action.

events.watch: Jobs upside = yields up = SOXX pressure (Jun 5 mechanism)
events.bear: Hot print near a catalyst = elevated crash risk
events.action_bull: No macro headwind. Focus on tape healing.
events.action_bear: Hot jobs + high TNX + SOXX already stressed = Jun 5-style vulnerability.

events.watch: WFE spending growth outlook (CEO Dickerson raised from 20% to >30% in May); token demand commentary
events.bull: 'WFE growth accelerating further'; DRAM/HBM capacity tight
events.action_bull: WFE upgrade = multi-year capex cycle intact.
events.action_bear: Walk-back from >30% WFE growth = major reversal signal.

events.watch: Q3 guide was ~$29.4B; AI revenue commentary — THIS CAUSED THE JUN 5 −10.4% CRASH
events.bull: Raises Q4 guide; AI segment raises; hyperscaler commentary strong
events.bear: Refuses to raise again (triggered Jun 5 crash); any AI softening
events.action_bull: Previous in-line report caused −10.4% crash. A raise here would be a major sentiment reversal.
events.action_bear: Second consecutive no-raise = pattern confirmed. Expect −10 to −15% event.

events.watch: Revenue vs $43.58B consensus; HBM shipment volumes; DRAM/NAND pricing outlook; Q1 FY27 guide
events.bull: Revenue beat + raised guide; HBM allocation sold out
events.bear: Revenue miss; softness in HBM pricing or allocation
events.action_bull: MU confirming HBM demand = SOXX fundamental thesis intact. Key regime-change confirmation.
events.action_bear: MU miss = memory cycle turn. HBM pricing softness would be the first crack in the AI spending thesis.
